title: Extended BACK-NET Website List
wikitext:

'''Meta Notice:''' This is a collaborative article! Everyone is allowed (and encouraged) to add their own additions to the article! Keep in mind that the list is formatted in alphabetical order.
[[Phenomenon 2|BACK-NET]] contains a variety of websites, both human-made and naturally occurring. Due to the massive amount of websites not allowing them all to be listed on BACK-NET's main page, a separate log was deemed necessary for cataloging them. The following is a collection of both popular and notable websites and those with interesting or anomalous characteristics. Wanderers are encouraged to add information about websites they have knowledge of to this chart. A website's domain is formatted as backnet-[level number of the server host]://[domain name].[top-level domain].


Almond Live
'''Domain:''' backnet0://almondlive.com
'''Source:''' Unknown Almond Live is a website of unknown source seemingly featuring live footage of a bottle of [[Object 1|Almond Water]] placed on a table in an unknown level. The footage is for the most part unchanging, although events such as the lights of the level flickering has shown it to be a video rather than a still image. It is rumored that [[Almond Water Inc]]. created the website as some form of bizarre advertisement.
Aurie Attack!
'''Domain:''' backnet1://aurieattack.io
'''Source:''' Unknown ''Aurie Attack!'' is a propaganda website created by the [[M.E.G.]] in order to simulate an invasion instigated by the [[Republic of Aurielle]]. It was originally made for the sake of military training and preparation but was publicized in order to spread anti-Auriellen beliefs and improve the image of the [[M.E.G.]] The website has been promptly banned in the colonies of [[Republic of Aurielle|Aurielle]], although they have made an off-brand version named ''Meggie Massacres '' that twists the storyline into a [[M.E.G.]] attack, taking much more brutal and grotesque imagery. This game has caused a great divide between hitherto neutral wanderers and has created further hatred between the two groups.
Frontnews
'''Domain:''' backnet0://frontnews.back.net
'''Source:''' Unknown Frontnews is a website that show news from the [[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms]]. Its creator is unknown, but it's possible that this website was created to manipulate humans.
[[Backchat (Collaborative Log)|Backchat]]
'''Domain:''' backnet4://back.chat
'''Source:''' Collaborative Formerly the Public Interdimensional Forum, and commonly nicknamed ∞chan (pronounced "infchan"), [[Backchat (Collaborative Log)|Backchat]] stands as the most popular social network in the entire Backrooms, similar in design and functionality to sites like 4chan. Around 95 million registered accounts exist on it (although most have not seen use for years), and it remains an easy way for one to reach out to other people and exchange survival tips in the otherwise isolated landscape of this dimension.
Back-Graph
'''Domain:''' backnet11://backgraph.net
'''Source:''' Collaborative Back-Graph is an online social network, where wanderers can publish photographs of levels, entities, phenomena or objects they find whilst exploring, without any moderation or restriction. Users can also create dedicated topics, which are typically used to identify unrecognized objects found around the Backrooms. Despite being one of the most popular social networks in the Backrooms, Back-Graph has faced controversy for users who publish strange photographs and go without punishment.
Backroom Crawlers
'''Domain:''' backnet7.5://backroomcrawlers.com
'''Source:''' Collaborative The recently made “Backrooms Crawlers” is a website designed and programmed by a group of friends. The purpose of the website is to discover and document new levels without the micromanagement of being under a large group. Despite the somewhat anarchistic foundation the website is intended for, it has managed to gain a small community of a thousand users within its recent creation. The community is welcoming to new members and is usually happy to help newer wanderers get the equipment they need to provide to the platform.
Backrooms Today Online
'''Domain:''' backnet399://backroomstoday.org
'''Source:''' [[Backrooms Today|Backrooms, Today]] Backrooms Today Online is an online news site created by Backrooms Today in 2003. The website features digital versions of Backrooms Today's print publications, as well as extra features such as videos and interactive charts. More recently, a forum has been included on the site for users to discuss news and current events, although it has been criticized as various groups have used the form to spread propaganda and disinformation.
Backvideo
'''Domain:''' backnet154://backvideo.net
'''Source:''' Collaborative Backvideo is a video sharing site similar to websites such as YouTube where wanderers can upload short video clips. Due to the hardware of BACK-NET terminals, these videos are typically low-resolution and take some time to load. The site has 10 million registered accounts, making it one of the largest in The Backrooms.
Clans.io
'''Domain:''' backnet29://clans.io
'''Source:''' Collaborative Clans.io, sometimes simply referred to as “Group Watch” is a collaborative, comprehensive list of known, documented groups. The site classifies communities by hostility, size and population, as well as overall stability. Who founded this database is unknown, though it is has became an informative site with wanderers being able to submit information. It is advised to take this site granted; as information can become quickly outdated or otherwise unreliable. Some controversies have emerged over time, with reports of bias regarding the sites managing body.
[[The Database]]
'''Domain:''' backnet0://thearchives.org
'''Source:''' Collaborative “[[The Database]],” also referred to as “The Archives” within its source code, is search engine of anomalous origin hosted on BACK-NET, seemingly designed to compile documentation regarding the Backrooms. Although it is a useful tool, one would be advised against taking it for granted; many webpages stored by the Database contain bias, and misinformation runs rampant without any moderation.
[[Object 10/Shroomsite|Shroomsite]]
'''Domain:''' backnet154://shroomsite.net
'''Source:''' Collaborative Shroomsite (although often known by its users as "DS": "Doin' Shrooms") is a collaborative forum where users share their personal innovations or "life-hacks" using [[Object 10|Backshrooms]]. The site has garnered a significant community of individuals addicted to Backshroom-derived substances, particularly involving Insanity Shrooms, Kharthic Shrooms, and Marigold Shroom mixtures. Much of the main feed consists of such substances in combination; however, there exists a category filter to allow wanderers to find helpful information regarding survival.
[[A.A.E.G./The_4_Files|The_4_Files]]
'''Domain:''' backnet1123://AustinFiles.ag
'''Source:''' Reports from the [[A.A.E.G.]] "[[A.A.E.G./The_4_Files|The_4_Files]]" is the official page of the [[A.A.E.G.]] where they publish information about their explorations and investigations of levels, phenomena, and entities. All—or most—of the information is public. Additionally, within the page, there is an online store belonging to the [[A.A.E.G.]] where you can buy various objects made by the [[A.A.E.G.]]
Entity Commentalog
'''Domain:''' backnet11.89://encom.jp
'''Source:''' Collaborative, mainly by Entity Commentary tulpents Entity Commentalog is a blog website with anyone can comment. A group of [[Entity 3|Smilers]] who inserted false information to Entity Watch also commented to this blog, through it was replied with "Don't think they wants individual informations of y'all"(sic).
Entity Watch
'''Domain:''' backnet4.3://entitywatch.org
'''Source:''' Collaborative, managed by the Anti-Entity Group Entity Watch is a website featuring information about different entities and the means to survive and fight them. Originally created by the A.E.G, the website has since become a collaborative effort, with individual wanderers being able to submit information to the site.
Free Backrooms Forum
'''Domain:''' backnet1://freebackroomsforum.chat
'''Source:''' Collaborative The Free Backrooms Forum, often abbreviated to FBF is an online forum where members discuss various conspiracy theories. Theories discussed on the forum range from accusations of political corruption among major groups to more esoteric topics such as the nature of the Backrooms as some form of simulation. While many of the more outlandish claims made on the site are nonsense, some, such as that of [[The First]] being a designation for multiple individuals instead of a single person have been proven true.
The Frontrooms Game
'''Domain:''' backnet477://frontroomsgame.io
'''Source:''' Unknown The Frontrooms Game, as its name implies, is an online game based on a depiction of [[The Frontrooms]]. In the game, one navigates poorly rendered, procedurally generated versions of various locations in The Frontrooms, such as cities, forests, and suburbs. The game has no aim or story and features no depictions of humans or animals. Despite the website stating the purpose of the game is to "give users feelings of home", players have described the atmosphere of the game to be more akin to the liminal spaces of The Backrooms.
LimAI
'''Domain:''' backnet27://liminal.ai
'''Source:''' Natural Liminal.ai, more commonly known as Liminal AI or simply LimAI is an AI chatbot seemingly naturally generated by BACK-NET, being one of the most complex naturally generated sites. However, rather than being useful, the chatbot found on LimAI responds to prompts with seemingly unrelated non-sequiturs or random strings of text in various languages. When asked about its nature or other details about the AI, the site will display a series of flashing colors before kicking the user off the site and blocking the terminal from accessing it.
PathFinder
'''Domain:''' backnet1://pathfinder.org
'''Source:''' [[M.E.G.|Major Explorer Group]] PathFinder is a simple website acting as an extension to the [[The Database|Database]]. It allows one to view how to exit the level they are on, and even the locations of exists, although the rapidly shifting nature of levels and poor coverage in unsafe or unstable levels makes the usefulness of this feature dubious. Recently, PathFinder introduced a feature listing the easiest path between two levels, the finding of which had previously been a common game or challenge among [[Backchat (Collaborative Log)|Backchat]] Users.

Virus Video
'''Domain:''' backnet151://backvideo.com
'''Source:''' Mutated smilers. Virus video is a fake version of Backvideo, created by Mutated Smilers in Level 151. Humans are unable to upload to Virus Virus, which pretends to be Backvideo. Contained on Virus Video are fake "entity survival" guides, mostly about smilers. The content gives detrimental tips that are the inverse of what you should do, as well as telling you to go to a level with smilers inside. It was created by said mutated smiles to get more people. Virus Video can be told apart via the number being wrong and it ending in .com rather than .net. Going to this website will also make the terminal explode after 10 minutes.
Y7FF73UHE
'''Domain:''' backnet7://Y7FF73UHE.com
'''Source:''' Collaborative Y7FF73UHE, or simply referred to as Y7F, is a collaborative video sharing platform. Often classified as a shock website, Y7F freely hosts real footage of extreme violence, gore, war crimes, and any sort of similar thing. It is unknown who hosts the website, though theories have emerged it is a honeypot used by the [[Backrooms Investigation Foundation]].
Backblog
'''Domain:''' backnet20://backblog.com
'''Source:''' Votrumuve & Monteith Backblog, often referred to as "Backlog" is a blogging website hosted by two wanderers: Ethan Votrumuve and Erron Monteith. Backblog is a website for wanderers to share their experiences with the backrooms as well as document their findings. During the The Flicker this website was used by The Major Explorer group to contact other groups as well as colonies.
𝛌elp
'''Domain:''' backnet?://𝛌elp.com
'''Source:''' Collaborative, mostly [[H.K. (Count the Stars!)|H.K.]] 𝛌elp, also known as 𝛌elp.com, is a review-based website imitating that of Yelp. The core difference is that 𝛌elp is dedicated entirely to reviewing Backrooms levels. This is also the main outlet for reviews by [[H.K. (Count the Stars!)|H.K.]], and as such should typically be avoided. For unknown reasons, this particular website tends to consistently have a stronger internet connection than others, meaning that it may be used as a last resort to glean information.
=====
Florae
===== '''Domain:''' backnet6://Floraehub.com
'''Source:''' Collaborative Florae, also known as FloraeHub, is an online wiki-based website created by Janice Green of the Backrooms Botanical Authority to help document the various florae of the Backrooms. Florae primarily concentrates on plants native to the Backrooms, although it has various pages on Frontrooms flora that can be found in the Backrooms. The website is relatively miniature, hosting only around one hundred articles, but most of these go heavily into depth. Currently, the most popular articles are [[Object 10|Back-shrooms]], [[Level 83|Rose-blood]], and [[Level 164 (Joke)|Level 164]].
=====
Backvid
===== '''Domain:''' backnet6://backvid.com
'''Source:''' Collaborative Backvid also known as Backvid.com is a collaborative website similar to youtube in the frontrooms, albeit way more glitchy. The website is hosted and maintained by a group of 15 anonymous wanderers. Backvid's interface is similar to youtube except with a black background. Users are able to upload videos as well as comment, the website is incredibly glitchy and prone to crashing. Pop-up adds are common on Backvid and pose a significant threat to ones device as some host malicious malware. Since its creation in 2001, the website has ganed a huge following, amassing approximately 300,000 users within 4 years and 900,000 users in the present day.
Orphiſt
'''Domain:''' backnet?://orphist.fun
'''Source:''' [[Caius Taggert|The Partygoer Jamboree]] (taken down) Orphist, stylised as Orphiſt, was a popular website run by [[Caius Taggert]] under a hidden URL identifier, which provided many forms of entertainment such as video games and streaming services up until 2024. The [[Party Crashers]] discovered that the countless individuals who created accounts on the site unknowingly downloaded malware to their devices, which subtly thieved and purged the victims' personal information, granting the Jamboree access to some of the most confidential information on the [[Phenomenon 2|BACK-NET]]. Following this horrific discovery, the Crashers immediately cooperated with officials from the [[M.E.G.]] and [[Backroom Colonists]] to take down the deceptive website, but the damage that had already been done could not be negated.
SaFeVid-25
'''Domain:''' backnetfun://safevidstreaming.fun
'''Source:''' [[Caius Taggert|The Partygoer Jamboree]] In late 2025, [[Caius Taggert]] announced "SaFeViD-25", a new video streaming service (similar to Frontrooms Netflix or Prime Video) that promised to be genuine, until its predecessor Orphist. Most assumed it was just another scam, and [[the Party Pooper]] even analysed the service's name to reveal that it likely stood for "'''Sa'''nguine '''Fe'''stivus '''Vi'''rus '''D'''isease 20'''25'''" (in reference to the formatting of CoViD-19), and used this as evidence that Sir Taggert was attempting to start a pandemic. However, when [[Party Crashers]] decided to investigate the site upon its release, they found it completely devoid of malware, seemingly just being an innocent streaming service like Taggert had made it out to be. However, the Party Host is never to be trusted, and the investigation continues...
=====
Mercury Retrograde Chart
===== '''Domain:''' backnet296://mercuryretrograde.com
'''Source:''' Natural The Mercury Retrograde Chart (M.R.C.) is a music chart compiled collaboratively on behalf of the Backrooms's music industry, listing the highest-selling songs across the Wake, based upon data collected from physical sales, paid-for downloads and streaming. It is commonly used as a definitive source of the popularity of songs and artists among wanderers. The host of the website is yet unknown; it appears to be naturally generated by the [[Phenomenon 2|BACK-NET]].
The Backrooms Level List
'''Domain:''' backnet72://yourlistoflevels.com
'''Source:''' Collaborative The Backrooms Level List is a mostly user-generated website that contains a series of lists consisting of different levels organized in numerical/alphabetical order. There are currently 3 known lists that can be found: the Level List, the Sub-Level list, and the Anomalous List. The first list consists of every documented numbered level of the Backrooms, the second list consists of every documented sub-level of the Backrooms, and the last list consists of every anomalous level that has been documented. Even though the website is mostly collaborative, there have been reported cases of levels being seemingly added to the lists without anyone doing so. The creator of this site is unknown, however people managed to find a now deleted account in the edit history of the list.
7h37ru7h.net
'''Domain:''' [[Level 248/7h37ru7h.net|backnet248://7h37ru7h.net]]
'''Source:''' Illegal TheSecret is a website on the dark web from [[Level 248]] that aims to hack into and access hidden information from the [[Royal Exploration Unit]] government. The software they use is on their site. Any attempts to take it down results in a shutdown of the level's power grid so the site is up, with the government frankly unwilling to shut the website down at the cost of the nation's power. Investigations are ongoing.
=====
M.E.G. Undisclosed Database
===== '''Domain:''' backnet?://MEG.com
'''Source:''' Privatized The M.E.G. Undisclosed Database is a privatized version of the official [[Backrooms Wiki| Public Database]]. The Undisclosed Database contains information on heavily undocumented, unsafe or otherwise dangerous levels which may jeopardize public safety if released. Numerous hidden files and classified documents are stored within this system. Access is limited strictly to authorized M.E.G. operatives and requires a personalized security code issued on the day of employment. Any operatives found to have shared, leaked, or lost their access code will face severe disciplinary action.
=====
Aurielle Netus Agenium
===== '''Domain:''' backnet339://Ci.to
'''Source:''' Privatized The Aurielle Network Agency (tr. Aurielle Netus Agenium, acronym ANA) is an organization owned and operated by the government of the [[Republic of Aurielle]]. The organization delivers internet connection to the citizens of Aurielle, using servers held at an undisclosed location in [[Level 339|Volucris]]. An alternative domain name, Corsiusci.to, is used for the armed forces, and in a similar manner the domain name Peamci.to is used for the government. The ANA is an important pillar of Aurielle’s digital presence.
=====
LimTunes
===== '''Domain:''' backnet833://lim.tunes.net
'''Source:''' The Sounds of Liminality Organization LimTunes is a nonprofit music streaming service aiming to be similar to other streaming services in the Frontrooms like Spotify, Apple Music, etc. LimTunes garners its selections of sounds through donated .mp3's and downloaded songs from wanderers. The service runs on BackNET, allowing it to be used anywhere where there is an Internet connection, and allows downloading of song files to access songs beyond the scope of Wi-Fi.
